perturbation.original_text: 3.1 LIMITED WARRANTY. Any products purchased from SCAM which become defective will be Warranted to ANUBIS for a period of 24 months from the date of shipment to ANUBIS. Such warranted units will be repaired or replaced by SCAM.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 LIMITED WARRANTY. Any products purchased from SCAM which become defective will be Warranted to ANUBIS for a period of 12 months from the date of shipment to ANUBIS, unless otherwise specified in writing. Such warranted units will be repaired or replaced by SCAM at their sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original warranty period is clearly defined as 24 months. The modified version reduces it to 12 months, but introduces an exception ('unless otherwise specified in writing'), and gives SCAM sole discretion in repair or replacement. The contradiction lies in the uncertainty of the actual warranty period and the repair/replacement obligation. This will create an In-Text contradiction against  6.2 PRODUCT QUALITY. SCAM represents and warrants to ANUBIS that the Products will be of good quality in all respects, that the materials comprising the Products shall not be defective, and that the Products sold to ANUBIS hereunder shall operate in conformance with SCAM's specifications with respect to such Products as set forth in any literature or packaging accompanying or related to such Products.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE 3. TITLE, RISK OF LOSS AND WWARRANTY LIMITATION
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 PRODUCT QUALITY. SCAM represents and warrants to ANUBIS that the Products will be of good quality in all respects, that the materials comprising the Products shall not be defective, and that the Products sold to ANUBIS hereunder shall operate in conformance with SCAM's specifications with respect to such Products as set forth in any literature or packaging accompanying or related to such Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 24-month warranty. The changed text reduces this to 12 months 'unless otherwise specified in writing' and gives SCAM sole discretion on repair/replacement. This directly contradicts the intent of section 6.2, which warrants the products will be of good quality and operate according to SCAM's specifications. The 'unless otherwise specified' and 'sole discretion' clauses introduce ambiguity and uncertainty about the actual warranty period and the repair/replacement obligation, conflicting with the quality and operational conformance guarantees of section 6.2. This fits the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 TERM. The term of this agreement shall initially be twelve months commencing as of the date and year first above written unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Article 8. After such twelve month period, the agreement shall continue on a year to year basis unless either party shall give written notice to the other within sixty (60) days from the end of such period or unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions of this paragraph 8.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 TERM. The term of this agreement shall initially be twelve months commencing as of the date and year first above written unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Article 8. After such twelve month period, the agreement shall automatically renew for successive one-year terms, unless both parties mutually agree in writing to terminate the agreement at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows for year-to-year continuation unless either party gives a 60-day written notice. The modified version introduces automatic renewal unless *both* parties agree to terminate, and changes the notice period to 30 days. This changes the termination conditions and creates conflict on what requirements are necessary to terminate it or not. Since  9.1 COMPLETE AGREEMENT; MODIFICATIONS. This agreement and any documents referred to herein or executed contemporaneously herewith constitute the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may not be amended, altered or modified except by a writing signed by the parties. This agreement supersedes all agreements, representations, warranties, statements, promises and understandings, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof. is supposed to be the entire agreement, but a contradiction is created here.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 COMPLETE AGREEMENT; MODIFICATIONS. This agreement and any documents referred to herein or executed contemporaneously herewith constitute the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may not be amended, altered or modified except by a writing signed by the parties. This agreement supersedes all agreements, representations, warranties, statements, promises and understandings, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in section 8.1 changes the termination conditions of the agreement. The original text allows termination with a 60-day notice from either party, while the modified text requires mutual agreement and a 30-day notice. This directly contradicts section 9.1, which states that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement and can only be modified by a writing signed by both parties. The change in termination conditions introduces an inconsistency with the 'entire agreement' clause by altering the requirements for termination and notice period. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as it breaks the original terms of the agreement according to 9.1.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3 This Agreement will apply to all confidential and proprietary information disclosed by one party to the other party, including information listed in Exhibit A and other information which the disclosing party identifies in writing as confidential before or within thirty days after disclosure to the receiving party ("CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION")
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 This Agreement will apply to all confidential and proprietary information disclosed by one party to the other party, excluding information listed in Exhibit A, unless the disclosing party notifies the receiving party orally at the time of disclosure that such information is considered confidential, and confirms this in writing within sixty days after disclosure ("CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION").
perturbation.explanation: The original text includes information in Exhibit A and requires written notification within 30 days. The change excludes Exhibit A unless orally notified at disclosure + written confirmation in 60 days. The contradiction arises over what qualifies as confidential information and the timing/method of notification. This introduces and in-text contradiction because in 5.1 MAINTENANCE OF CONFIDENTIALITY. ANUBIS acknowledges that the processes used by SCAM to develop and produce the Products involve confidential information and data of substantial value to SCAM which value would be impaired if said information and data were disclosed. ANUBIS agrees (1) to safeguard the Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this agreement (2) not to use the Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this agreement for any purpose other than (to the extent necessary) to further the sale of and promotion of Products and (3) to cooperate in any lawsuit involving the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 MAINTENANCE OF CONFIDENTIALITY. ANUBIS acknowledges that the processes used by SCAM to develop and produce the Products involve confidential information and data of substantial value to SCAM which value would be impaired if said information and data were disclosed. ANUBIS agrees (1) to safeguard the Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this agreement (2) not to use the Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this agreement for any purpose other than (to the extent necessary) to further the sale of and promotion of Products and (3) to cooperate in any lawsuit involving the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 5.3 alters the definition of "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" by excluding Exhibit A unless specific oral and written notifications are given. This contradicts 5.1, which obligates ANUBIS to safeguard all Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to the agreement. The change in 5.3 introduces ambiguity and creates a conflict because it is now unclear whether information in Exhibit A is automatically considered confidential (as it was in the original text and as might be implied by 5.1) or only confidential if the new notification procedure is followed. This inconsistency creates a direct and impactful contradiction that would cause confusion and potential disputes over what information ANUBIS is obligated to safeguard under 5.1, thus falling under the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction type.

perturbation.changed_text: 7. Sales Territory: Initially Whole Europe, but SCAM reserves the right to exclude specific countries with a 15-day written notice to ANUBIS.
perturbation.explanation: The original Sales Territory is defined as 'Whole Europe.' The modification allows SCAM to exclude countries with only 15 days' notice. This contradicts the clear definition of the Sales Territory and creates uncertainty regarding ANUBIS's exclusive rights. This creates an in-text contradiction to 2.1 EXTENT OF REPRESENTATION RIGHTS. Under the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement, SCAM hereby appoints ANUBIS, and ANUBIS accepts such appointment as the representative to sell the Products to customers in the Sales territory. Unless specifically otherwise authorized in writing by SCAM, ANUBIS shall not directly or indirectly by any means whatsoever contact or solicit any customer or customers outside of Sales Territory or establish any branch or depot for the purpose of selling the Products outside of the Sales Territory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 EXTENT OF REPRESENTATION RIGHTS. Under the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement, SCAM hereby appoints ANUBIS, and ANUBIS accepts such appointment as the representative to sell the Products to customers in the Sales territory. Unless specifically otherwise authorized in writing by SCAM, ANUBIS shall not directly or indirectly by any means whatsoever contact or solicit any customer or customers outside of Sales Territory or establish any branch or depot for the purpose of selling the Products outside of the Sales Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the sales territory as 'Whole Europe.' The changed text allows SCAM to exclude countries with 15 days' notice, creating uncertainty and contradicting the original definition. This directly conflicts with section 2.1, which grants ANUBIS representation rights for the 'Sales territory.' The ability for SCAM to unilaterally reduce the Sales Territory creates a direct contradiction with the scope of representation rights granted in 2.1, making it unclear and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Placing order for Quarter 1 a. ANUBIS shall place the order for 50,000 units 6 weeks prior to mass production for buying long term components.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Placing order for Quarter 1 a. ANUBIS should endeavor to place the order for approximately 50,000 units 6 weeks prior to mass production, but the exact quantity and timing are subject to change based on market demand.
perturbation.explanation: The original term states ANUBIS 'shall' place the order for 50,000 units with a specific timeline. The modified version uses 'should endeavor' and introduces 'market demand' as a variable that could change the quantity and timing. This creates uncertainty about ANUBIS's obligations regarding order placement. This contradicts against 7.1 PROMOTION. To diligently use it best efforts to introduce, promote the sale of, and obtain orders for, the Products in the Sales Territory. ANUBIS obligations of placing an order.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 PROMOTION. To diligently use it best efforts to introduce, promote the sale of, and obtain orders for, the Products in the Sales Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated ANUBIS to place an order for 50,000 units, whereas the changed text weakens this obligation to 'should endeavor' and introduces flexibility based on 'market demand.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires ANUBIS to 'diligently use its best efforts...to obtain orders for...the Products'. By making the order quantity and timing uncertain, the changed text undermines ANUBIS's responsibility to diligently obtain orders, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
